mnemonics: Admissible Heuristics are Always Accurate (never overestimate).
keyPoints: A heuristic function estimates the cost to reach the goal from a given state.
An admissible heuristic never overestimates the true cost to the goal, ensuring A* search finds an optimal path.
A consistent (or monotonic) heuristic satisfies the triangle inequality, ensuring A* is efficient and finds the optimal path when it expands a node.
commonMistakes: Assuming a more complex heuristic is always better; sometimes a simpler, computationally cheaper heuristic is more efficient overall.
Using an inadmissible heuristic for A* search, which guarantees the solution found will not be optimal.
Confusing 'consistent' with 'admissible'; all consistent heuristics are admissible, but not all admissible heuristics are consistent.
